title: Name cosmological status age and weapon remain unresolved
source_observation: The poem identifies Kanchanan through translated Vidyadhara Vinjayan and sky-world language but modern equivalents and material design are uncertain
risk: The production could invent a racial species biography exact age or pseudo-historical sword system
decision: Keep terminology provisional and approve casting costume VFX travel and weapon design only after Tamil religious-history and stunt review
rationale: Prevents fantasy shorthand from becoming false historical certainty
required_review: Classical Tamil; history of religions; casting; costume; VFX; arms; publicity

title: Long care is real but creates no ownership
source_observation: Kanchanan supports Kayasandihai during the curse identifies help and returns during Indra festivals
risk: Sympathy for caregiving could be used to imply that she owes recognition obedience intimacy or sexual access
decision: Show tenderness effort and loyalty while making consent identity and autonomy independent of past care
rationale: Preserves complexity without converting sacrifice into entitlement
required_review: Gender studies; coercive-control review; screenplay; actor preparation

title: Kayasandihai retains an independent life cure choice and death
source_observation: Kayasandihai narrates her curse receives food directs Manimekalai departs independently and dies beyond Kanchanan's knowledge
risk: She could be reduced to a wife-shaped trigger for male jealousy and remorse
decision: Give her full narrative agency and do not invent infidelity passivity or a reunion that the poem denies
rationale: Protects the woman whose identity the disguise borrows
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender studies; Kayasandihai dossier; screenplay

title: Mistaken identity is built from limited observation not madness
source_observation: Kanchanan sees his wife's form unexpected behaviour conversation with the prince and a nighttime return but lacks decisive identity and context information
risk: The scene could use possession magic irrational insanity or audience mockery to avoid examining interpretation
decision: Separate observed facts inferences missing questions and chosen actions in blocking performance and edit
rationale: Makes the causal error intelligible while retaining responsibility

title: Jealousy and wounded expectation do not justify lethal certainty
source_observation: Kanchanan interprets apparent rejection and royal intimacy as betrayal
risk: Romantic grammar could present jealousy as devotion or violence as understandable marital honour
decision: Frame jealousy as fear humiliation possessiveness and refusal to tolerate uncertainty and never as proof of love
rationale: Protects consent principles and prevents honour-violence romanticisation
required_review: Gender-violence specialist; ethics; screenplay; music; publicity

title: Concealment and opportunities to stop remain visible
source_observation: The poem says Kanchanan enters with a sword hides and waits before the prince's midnight return
risk: A rapid reaction cut could make the killing seem reflexive accidental or unavoidable
decision: Preserve weapon possession concealment duration spatial knowledge and alternatives to striking
rationale: Maintains causal and moral clarity without inventing a modern trial
required_review: Classical Tamil; stunt; blocking; editor; legal-history review

title: The killing is not a heroic duel or defence of marriage
source_observation: The supplied translation describes Kanchanan rising behind the returning prince and using the sword
risk: Commercial action staging could invent an equal fight self-defence or noble warning that softens responsibility
decision: Keep the action brief asymmetric and text-controlled and verify the exact wound before choreography
rationale: Honours the source and avoids aesthetic reward for lethal jealousy
required_review: Classical Tamil; fight choreographer; weapons; safeguarding; director

title: Udayakumaran's pursuit and Kanchanan's killing remain separate responsibilities
source_observation: The prince returns despite refusal while Kanchanan independently interprets and kills
risk: One man's wrongdoing could be used to absolve the other or form a romantic triangle around a woman's body
decision: Show the prince's boundary violation and Kanchanan's lethal judgement as distinct choices inside a shared causal chain
rationale: Preserves multi-agent accountability and Manimekalai's autonomy
required_review: Screenplay; gender studies; ethics; Udayakumaran continuity

title: Divine revelation explains fate while preserving accountability
source_observation: The Kanthil Pavai reveals identity Kayasandihai's death and Udayakumaran's fate while calling Kanchanan's deed cruel and predicting consequence
risk: Selective use of karma could turn murder into predestination or divine acquittal
decision: Retain both causal fate and explicit moral ownership and stage the revelation in sequential destabilising facts
rationale: Protects the epic's karmic world without erasing present choice
required_review: Buddhist studies; classical Tamil; performance; edit; dialogue

title: Sorrowful departure is not completed redemption
source_observation: The poem says Kanchanan leaves for the sky world feeling sorry but supplies no restitution reconciliation or later reform
risk: A remorse close-up or invented penance could centre the killer and close the consequences prematurely
decision: End the textual arc with knowledge sorrow and unresolved consequence and label any later scene as interpretation
rationale: Preserves the poem's incompleteness and the harmed characters' centrality
required_review: Classical Tamil; ethics; screenplay; editor; publicity
